edits.new: NUM_WORKERS = 0  # CPU-safe, avoid shared memory issues

rationale: The RuntimeError 'unable to allocate shared memory(shm)' is caused by PyTorch DataLoader workers (num_workers=4) trying to allocate shared memory tensors in /dev/shm, which is too small in this containerized environment. Setting num_workers=0 disables multiprocess data loading, eliminating shared memory allocation entirely. This is safe for CPU-only evaluation and does not change any semantic behavior (same data, same model, same scoring).
